terms.definition: A bacterial disease causing demineralisation and progressive destruction of the hard tissues of the tooth.
terms.exampleSentence: Bitewing radiographs revealed interproximal dental caries on the lower molars.
terms.contextNotes: Singular noun used with singular verb in clinical writing.

terms.definition: Chronic inflammation of the periodontium causing attachment loss, bone loss, and eventual tooth loss.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient was diagnosed with generalised stage III periodontitis.
terms.contextNotes: Staged I–IV and graded A–C in current classification.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the gingiva without loss of attachment, typically reversible with plaque control.
terms.exampleSentence: Marginal gingivitis was noted around the lower anterior teeth.
terms.contextNotes: Reversible if biofilm is removed and home care improves.

terms.definition: A localised collection of pus within the tooth or surrounding tissues caused by bacterial infection.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient presented with a fluctuant dental abscess in the lower left quadrant.
terms.contextNotes: Drainage is essential; antibiotics alone are inadequate.

terms.definition: Misalignment of the teeth or incorrect relationship between the upper and lower dental arches.
terms.exampleSentence: Class II division 1 malocclusion was diagnosed and orthodontic referral made.
terms.contextNotes: Classified by Angle into Class I, II, III.

terms.definition: The condition in which a tooth fails to erupt fully due to obstruction by bone, soft tissue, or another tooth.
terms.exampleSentence: Mesioangular impaction of the lower third molar was confirmed on the OPG.
terms.contextNotes: Common with third molars and upper canines.

terms.definition: Tooth wear caused by tooth-to-tooth contact, often from bruxism.
terms.exampleSentence: Marked attrition of the incisal edges was consistent with nocturnal bruxism.
terms.contextNotes: Distinguish from erosion (chemical) and abrasion (mechanical non-tooth).

terms.definition: Tooth wear caused by mechanical forces from objects other than teeth, such as toothbrushing.
terms.exampleSentence: Cervical abrasion lesions were noted on the buccal surfaces of the premolars.
terms.contextNotes: Often linked to aggressive horizontal brushing.

terms.definition: Loss of dental hard tissue caused by chemical processes not involving bacteria, typically dietary acids or reflux.
terms.exampleSentence: Palatal erosion of the upper incisors raised suspicion of gastric reflux.
terms.contextNotes: Aetiology may be intrinsic (reflux) or extrinsic (diet).

terms.definition: Short, sharp pain from exposed dentine in response to thermal, chemical, or tactile stimuli.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient reported dentine hypersensitivity to cold drinks for several months.
terms.contextNotes: Managed with desensitising toothpastes and fluoride varnish.

terms.definition: Malignant neoplasm arising in the oral cavity, most commonly squamous cell carcinoma.
terms.exampleSentence: An indurated ulcer on the lateral tongue raised concerns for oral cancer.
terms.contextNotes: Tobacco, alcohol, and HPV are major risk factors.

terms.definition: A white patch on the oral mucosa that cannot be wiped off and is not attributable to any other condition.
terms.exampleSentence: A homogeneous leukoplakia on the buccal mucosa was scheduled for biopsy.
terms.contextNotes: Considered a potentially malignant disorder.

terms.definition: A fungal infection of the oral mucosa caused by Candida species, often Candida albicans.
terms.exampleSentence: Pseudomembranous oral candidiasis presented as removable white plaques on the palate.
terms.contextNotes: Common in immunocompromised and denture wearers.

terms.definition: Recurrent vesicular eruption around the lips caused by herpes simplex virus type 1.
terms.exampleSentence: Treatment of the elective procedure was deferred due to active herpes labialis.
terms.contextNotes: Aciclovir cream may shorten the course if applied early.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the dental pulp, classified as reversible or irreversible.
terms.exampleSentence: Lingering pain to cold suggested irreversible pulpitis of the lower molar.
terms.contextNotes: Irreversible pulpitis requires endodontic treatment or extraction.

terms.definition: Involuntary grinding or clenching of the teeth, often during sleep.
terms.exampleSentence: A nocturnal occlusal splint was prescribed to manage the patient's bruxism.
terms.contextNotes: Linked to stress and sleep disorders.

terms.definition: Persistent unpleasant odour of the breath, often originating from the oral cavity.
terms.exampleSentence: Investigation of the halitosis revealed heavy tongue coating and active periodontitis.
terms.contextNotes: 85–90% of cases are intra-oral in origin.

terms.definition: The subjective sensation of dry mouth, often associated with reduced salivary flow.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient reported xerostomia following commencement of antihypertensive therapy.
terms.contextNotes: Increases caries risk; manage with saliva substitutes.

terms.definition: Abnormal movement of a tooth in its socket, graded from 1 to 3 clinically.
terms.exampleSentence: Grade 2 tooth mobility was recorded on the lower right central incisor.
terms.contextNotes: Often a late sign of advanced periodontal disease.

terms.definition: Mottling or hypomineralisation of enamel caused by excessive fluoride intake during tooth development.
terms.exampleSentence: Mild fluorosis presented as faint white striations across the upper incisors.
terms.contextNotes: Risk only during enamel formation in childhood.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the soft tissues surrounding a partially erupted tooth, classically the third molar.
terms.exampleSentence: Pericoronitis of the lower right third molar caused trismus and pain.
terms.contextNotes: Local irrigation and analgesia first-line; antibiotics if systemic signs.

terms.definition: A common, painful, recurrent ulcer of the oral mucosa with a clearly defined erythematous halo.
terms.exampleSentence: Multiple aphthous ulcers were present on the labial mucosa.
terms.contextNotes: Self-limiting in 7–14 days for minor lesions.

terms.definition: Inflammation of the tongue, often producing a red, smooth, swollen appearance.
terms.exampleSentence: Atrophic glossitis raised suspicion of vitamin B12 deficiency.
terms.contextNotes: May be a sign of nutritional deficiency.

terms.definition: A chronic immune-mediated mucocutaneous condition that can produce white reticular lesions in the mouth.
terms.exampleSentence: Bilateral reticular lichen planus was noted on the buccal mucosa.
terms.contextNotes: Erosive form has a small malignant transformation risk.

terms.definition: Restricted opening of the mouth, often due to muscle spasm, infection, or trauma.
terms.exampleSentence: Post-operative trismus limited interincisal opening to 18 mm.
terms.contextNotes: Severe trismus may indicate spreading infection.

terms.definition: A break in the structure of a tooth, ranging from enamel cracks to complete root fracture.
terms.exampleSentence: A vertical root fracture was suspected on the upper second premolar.
terms.contextNotes: Classified by Ellis or Andreasen schemes.

terms.definition: Complete displacement of a tooth out of its socket, typically due to trauma.
terms.exampleSentence: The avulsed permanent incisor was reimplanted within 30 minutes.
terms.contextNotes: Store in milk or saline if reimplantation is delayed.

terms.definition: Mineralised dental plaque adhering to the tooth surface, also known as tartar.
terms.exampleSentence: Heavy subgingival calculus deposits were noted on the lingual aspect of the lower incisors.
terms.contextNotes: Cannot be removed by toothbrushing alone.

terms.definition: A soft biofilm of bacteria that adheres to tooth surfaces and is a primary cause of caries and gingivitis.
terms.exampleSentence: Disclosing solution highlighted heavy dental plaque along the gingival margin.
terms.contextNotes: Removed by mechanical brushing and interdental cleaning.

terms.definition: Change in tooth colour from normal due to extrinsic staining or intrinsic factors such as trauma or tetracycline.
terms.exampleSentence: Intrinsic tooth discolouration following non-vital trauma was discussed with the patient.
terms.contextNotes: Differentiate intrinsic vs extrinsic before planning treatment.
